title: Cairn record (v0)
description: A nanopublication-shaped, content-addressed, signed knowledge artifact. One envelope for claims (verbs) and entities (nouns).

id.description: Trusty URI = tt: + base64url(SHA-256(JCS(content))); empty only on an unminted draft.

assertion.description: What is claimed / what exists. Open object; when a claim/source declares Trust-Ladder or grounding fields they must conform to $defs below.

derivedFrom.description: Upstream Cairn Trusty URIs this record derives from (the non-independence edge set).

allOf.$comment: Faithfulness invariant: an entailment-checked/accepted (L4/L5) claim MUST carry a grounding tuple. Bare/lower-rung claims stay valid so the envelope remains general.

verification.description: Trust-Ladder rung (internal research notes toolchest/01-provenance-and-nonindependence.md + internal notes): L1 Observed artifact · L2 Parsed artifact · L3 Attributed claim · L4 Entailment-checked claim · L5 Accepted library claim · L6 Analysis conclusion. The legacy 'unverified-fixture' value is intentionally NOT admissible: any record still carrying it fails validation.

grounding.description: The faithfulness tuple (internal research notes toolchest/02-claim-argument-mining.md:95-98; internal notes:122): every claim node carries (source_doc, char_span, extractor, entailment_label). `cairn ground` mechanically checks that source.assertion.excerpt[char_span] == quote and that source is in provenance.derivedFrom.

source.description: source_doc: Trusty URI of the epi:Source the span indexes into (must also appear in provenance.derivedFrom).

quote.description: The exact substring source.assertion.excerpt[start:end] — re-checkable on a fresh machine.

extractor.description: The tool/model/human that produced this span-grounded claim.

entailment_label.description: NLI verdict of span -> claim. Canonical SUPPORTS/REFUTES/NEI plus the richer ENTAILS/CONTRADICTS/IS-IRRELEVANT-TO.

source_sha256.description: Optional integrity bind: sha256 of source.assertion.excerpt at extraction time.
